PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Feb-17-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas DiFranco for Petitioner.

Mr. DiFranco stated that he received a call from Mr. Tom stating that they were no longer opposing appointment of his Client, that he sent the declination but that he has not received it back and that the Bank was not willing to release information on the bank accounts because other siblings were on the account. The Court granted the petition (subject to review of the order). Bond is not required.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-25-2011 Continued To Feb-09-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-14-2010 Continued To Jan-25-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-16-2010 Continued To Dec-14-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas DiFranco for Rhoda Hom. John Tom, Henry Tom and Shirley Lui were also present.

The Court stated that publication has occurred. Shirley Lui with the assistance of John Tom stated that she is willing to act as Executor. Mr. DiFranco stated that his Client was the only Beneficiary, that she has not received any information and that she does not get along with her Siblings. In addition, Mr. DiFranco stated that this could be a no asset estate and that there was only one bank (East West Bank) in question. The Court recommended that the Parties cooperate. The matter was continued to December 14, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to see if a competing petition is filed by Shirley Lui. If no competing petition is filed, the Court will proceed to appoint Rhoda. Mr. Tom stated that the Aunt will cooperate with obtaining information regarding the bank. The Court signed the order dispensing with notice to Kevin Hom.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
